specific_location: Just East of the intersection of Sedgwick County Roads 12 and 57
description: Overall the bird was brown above and quite buffy below. It was about six inches long, slightly smaller than the many Horned Larks in the area. Bill: Cnonical with a thin appearance Crown: Brown and finely streaked Face: Grayish auricular area with a distinct cheek outline broken at the rear and a buffy supercillium. There was a faint hint of a pale eye ring. Wings: Overall the wings were brown. The primary projection was long. There were two white wing bars the most prominent being the shoulder with a faint but noticable bar below. Back: Uniformly brown and streaked, the same color as the wings. Underparts: The breast and belly were completely buffy with faint streaking and the chin was pale. Tail: Only the outer two rectrices were white and seen only when the bird banked in flight.Behaviors: The bird was seen foraging in the short grass along the side of the road occasionally coming right to the edge of the vegetation where it was easily seen. It was not vocal while on the ground, the call was heard only in flight.Call: A dry rattle heard in flightPlumage: Basic
